## Appearances & Description

Lyn is a slender,(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) handsome(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) man with shoulder-length brown hair,(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1) a hard mouth,(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2) and restless eyes.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-3) Vain, reckless and hot-tempered,(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1) he has a wicked smile.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-4) Lyn is often venomous and mocking.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-5)(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-sample_8-0) The landless(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-sample_8-1) knight does not care for his elder brother, Lord [Lyonel Corbray](wiki:Lyonel_Corbray).(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-6)

Ser Lyn wields [Lady Forlorn](wiki:Lady_Forlorn) and also carries a dagger.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2) Coming from impoverished [House Corbray](wiki:House_Corbray),(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-3) he is always short of money.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-sample_8-2)

[Kevan Lannister](wiki:Kevan_Lannister), [Nestor Royce](wiki:Nestor_Royce), and [House Arryn guards](wiki:House_Arryn_guards) all consider Lyn to be dangerous,(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-7) with Kevan also considering him proud.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1) Lord [Robert Arryn](wiki:Robert_Arryn) dislikes Lyn.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-8) It is said that Lyn is "notoriously" uninterested in women.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-4)(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-sample_8-3) According to [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish), Lyn likes "boys".(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-9)

## Narrative Arc

### A Game of Thrones

Ser Lyn is one of Lady [Lysa Arryn](wiki:Lysa_Arryn)'s suitors after the death of Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn).(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-5) His squire is [Mychel Redfort](wiki:Mychel_Redfort).(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

Lyn attends the trial of [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) at the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie). He offers himself as champion of the [Vale](wiki:Vale_of_Arryn) when Tyrion demands [trial by combat](wiki:Trial_by_combat), but is rebuffed by Lysa.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1) Lyn suggests beheading Tyrion and sending his head to his brother, Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), but is again refused.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-6)

Following [Bronn](wiki:Bronn)'s victory over Ser [Vardis Egen](wiki:Vardis_Egen) in the trial,(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-7) Lyn escorts Tyrion and Bronn to the [Bloody Gate](wiki:Bloody_Gate), where he commands them never to return to the Vale.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)

### A Clash of Kings

Lyn remains a suitor of Lysa.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Racokappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)

### A Storm of Swords

Ser [Kevan Lannister](wiki:Kevan_Lannister) prefers having Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) ruling the Eyrie than any of Lysa's suitors, including Lyn.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-2)

Having [married Lysa](wiki:Wedding_of_Petyr_Baelish_and_Lysa_Arryn) and been named [Lord Protector of the Vale](wiki:Lord_Protector_of_the_Vale), Petyr travels to meet with the [Corbrays](wiki:House_Corbray).(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

### A Feast for Crows

Ser Lyn accompanies the [Lords Declarant](wiki:Lords_Declarant) to their parley with Petyr and young Lord [Robert Arryn](wiki:Robert_Arryn) at the Eyrie. Lord [Nestor Royce](wiki:Nestor_Royce) claims that Lyn rarely left the side of Lysa for nearly half a year. During the parley, Ser Lyn grows tired of the conversation and threatens Petyr with his sword, [Lady Forlorn](wiki:Lady_Forlorn), violating the laws of hospitality. Ser [Lothor Brune](wiki:Lothor_Brune) moves to intercept, but it is the intervention of the other Lords Declarant that causes Lyn to put up his sword. Lyn leaves the parley shortly thereafter.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-16)

Later, Petyr admits to [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) that Lyn is in his employ, and it is his task to feign hatred for Petyr and to join every conspiracy against him and inform on them. Petyr states that Lyn's price is [gold](wiki:Gold) and boys.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-17)

With Mychel Redfort now a knight, Lyn's new squire is a lad considered a lout by [Myranda Royce](wiki:Myranda_Royce).(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)

### The Winds of Winter

Prior to the [tourney](wiki:Tourney) for the [Brotherhood of Winged Knights](wiki:Brotherhood_of_Winged_Knights) at the [Gates of the Moon](wiki:Gates_of_the_Moon), Ser Lyn knocks down Ser [Owen](wiki:Owen_(knight)) during a practice match. The buxom Myranda japes that Lyn would not be interested in her because of her gender.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-sample_8-4)

## Quotes

### Quotes by Lyn

> The gods favor the man with the just cause, yet often that turns out to be the man with the surest sword. We all know who that is.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Ragot38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2)
>
> —Lyn to [Lysa Arryn](wiki:Lysa_Arryn)

> **Anya**: Mind your tongue, ser. **Lyn**: My tongue is my concern. Your ladyship should take care to mind her own. I have never taken kindly to chastisement, as any number of dead men could tell you.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-18)
>
> —[Anya Waynwood](wiki:Anya_Waynwood) and Lyn

> [My lady](wiki:Lady_Forlorn) has a thirst. Whenever she comes out to dance, she likes a drop of red.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-19)
>
> —Lyn to the [Lords Declarant](wiki:Lords_Declarant)

> **Alayne**: Well struck, Ser Lyn. Though I fear you've knocked poor Ser [Owen](wiki:Owen_(knight)) insensible. **Lyn**: He had no sense to start with, or he should not have tried me.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-sample_8-5)
>
> —[Alayne Stone](wiki:Sansa_Stark) and Lyn

> **Alayne**: [My father](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) is always pleased to be of service to one of Lord [Robert's](wiki:Robert_Arryn) leal [bannermen](wiki:Bannermen). I'm sure he would be most delighted to help broker a marriage for you as well, Ser Lyn. **Lyn**: How kind of him. But what need have I for heirs when I am landless and like to remain so, thanks to our [Lord Protector](wiki:Lord_Protector_of_the_Vale)? No. Tell your lord father I need none of his brood mares.(wiki:Lyn_Corbray.cite_ref-sample_8-6)
>
> —[Alayne Stone](wiki:Sansa_Stark) and Lyn
